message:
test(e2e): extend wills-estate diagnostic spec suite with OG, TOC, sticky-scroll, and multi-viewport screenshot captures

These `_diag-*` specs are diagnostic/one-shot Playwright scripts used to
capture evidence from the running dev server while iterating on the
wills-and-estate-planning essay. They are not part of the regression
suite; they emit images, scroll traces, and response bodies into
/tmp/wills-audit for manual inspection.

- tests/e2e/_diag-wills-viz.spec.ts: rewrite to use a broader viewport
  matrix (mobile 375, tablet 768, desktop 1280/1920) and assert that
  each target visualization actually rendered before snapshotting.
  Drops the two intermediate desktop sizes (1024, 1440) that never
  produced differentiated coverage and replaces the loose `test()`
  shape with one that uses `expect()` so failures surface in CI
  instead of emitting an empty screenshot.

- tests/e2e/_diag-og-render.spec.ts: fetches both
  `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image` and
  `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/twitter-image` via
  `page.request.get` and writes the raw PNG bytes to /tmp/wills-audit,
  logging HTTP status and byte count. Used to validate the new
  OG/Twitter image routes outside the normal Next.js OG preview flow.

- tests/e2e/_diag-wills-toc.spec.ts: walks the TOC links (`cost`,
  `what-is-it`, `setup`, `folder`, `tips`, `showcase`, `attorney`,
  `faq`, `pattern`) and verifies the scroll landing position lines up
  with each anchor target's `rect.top`. Captures documented
  `scrollY`/`docHeight`/`maxScroll` per link for jump-target
  regression debugging.

- tests/e2e/_diag-deliverables-fix.spec.ts: records the sticky-scroll
  behavior of the Deliverables-tree section — captures the rightTop,
  gridTop, and gridBottom values at each scroll step so we can prove
  whether the sticky container is actually pinned versus merely
  appearing to stick in screenshots.

These stay gated under the `_diag-` prefix so the normal E2E runner
(which targets unprefixed specs) does not pick them up.
